title: How to File Income Tax Grievance Online — e-Nivaran Portal 2026
meta_description: File income tax complaint on e-Nivaran portal at incometax.gov.in. Guide to resolve refund delays, wrong demand, TDS mismatch, and other tax grievances.

hero.one_liner: Got wrong tax demand, delayed refund, or TDS mismatch? File a grievance on e-Nivaran portal — free, online, response within 30 days

sections.heading: What is e-Nivaran Portal?
content: e-Nivaran (meaning 'redressal' in Hindi) is the official Income Tax Department's online grievance portal. It allows you to file complaints about income tax issues without visiting an office or paying any fee.
Your grievance is directed to the Central Processing Centre (CPC) in Bangalore, which investigates and responds within 30 days.

You can file grievances for: refund not received despite ITR filing, wrong tax demand raised, TDS mismatch between employer salary and IT records, PAN correction requests pending, ITR processing delays (beyond 30 days), rectification request not processed, or any other income tax department action you believe is incorrect.

The portal is integrated with your PAN records — the department can directly verify your ITR status, refund status, and demand notices while reviewing your grievance. This makes resolution faster than approaching the office in person.

rows.label: Refund pending 6+ months
rows.value: Check: Bank account details correct in ITR? ITR status 'Processed'? Any demand raised that's offsetting refund? File e-Nivaran with ITR acknowledgment + bank statement.

rows.label: Wrong tax demand raised
rows.value: Challenge in e-Nivaran with: ITR filed (show transcript), evidence of income source (salary slip, business records), proof of deduction already paid. Demand may be reversed if data error found.

rows.label: TDS mismatch
rows.value: TDS on salary shown different in IT records vs salary slip. Submit Form 26AS (shows all TDS) + salary slip + bank statement showing actual salary received.

rows.label: PAN details wrong
rows.value: Name, DOB, or address incorrect on PAN. Cannot change via e-Nivaran — update directly at NSDL website (₹107) or through your bank's PAN correction service.

rows.label: ITR not processed after 30 days
rows.value: ITR processing takes 30 days normally. After 30 days, file e-Nivaran with: ITR confirmation, proof of filing date, recent ITR status check. CPC will expedite.

sections.heading: How to File Income Tax Grievance — Step by Step

steps.title: Go to incometax.gov.in and login
steps.description: Visit incometax.gov.in. Login using your PAN and password (same login for ITR filing). If you don't have a password, click 'Forgot Password' to reset.

steps.title: Navigate to Grievance section
steps.description: After login, look for 'Services' menu → click 'Grievance' or search for 'e-Nivaran' on the homepage. You'll see 'Submit a Grievance' option. Click it.

steps.title: Select grievance type
steps.description: Choose from: Refund not received, Wrong demand raised, ITR processing issue, TDS mismatch, PAN correction pending, Rectification request not processed, Other. Be specific — if you choose wrong category, resolution may be delayed.

steps.title: Describe the issue clearly
steps.description: Write a clear, concise description (500-1000 characters). Include: assessment year(s) affected, amount involved (demand/refund), what happened, what you expect as resolution. Example: 'Refund of ₹45,000 for AY 2024-25 filed in July 2024, still not received. Bank account: XXXXX1234. Expected resolution: Refund to my savings account within 2 weeks.'

steps.title: Attach supporting documents
steps.description: Upload PDF/image files of: ITR acknowledgment (XML file from email or portal), ITR tax computation sheet, income tax challan receipts (if payment made), bank account statement (first page showing account details), salary slip or Form 16 if TDS issue, demand letter (if applicable). Max 5 files, 5 MB each.

steps.title: Submit and track
steps.description: Review all details, then click 'Submit'. You receive a grievance number (e.g., GR/2026/PAN12345/00123). Save this number. Go to 'Track Grievance' → enter this number to see status and CPC's response. You also get SMS/email updates.

sections.heading: Grievance Status Tracking & Response Timeline
rows: Your grievance received by CPC Bangalore
CPC team is reviewing your ITR, refund, or demand records
No action needed — wait for reply (15-25 days)
CPC has submitted response/resolution to the portal
Check the 'Reply' section on the portal — read CPC's decision
Your issue resolved (e.g., refund credited, demand withdrawn)
Verify refund in bank or demand notice withdrawn from IT portal
If unsatisfied, you can appeal to the Higher Authority or file RTI

sections.heading: Documents You Should Keep Ready
content: Before filing e-Nivaran, gather these documents so you can upload them quickly:
For refund issues:
ITR acknowledgment email from incometax.gov.in (shows filing date and assessment year). Bank statement or passbook (first page showing name, account number, IFSC code, branch).
ITR tax computation sheet (shows expected refund amount). Previous year's bank statement if refund was supposed to be credited to old account.
For wrong demand issues:
Demand letter from IT department (shows demand amount, assessment year, why demand was raised). Your ITR filing for that year (transcript).
Income proof: salary slips, Form 16, business registration certificate. Proof of any tax already paid: challan receipts, TDS certificates (Form 16A).
For TDS mismatch:
Form 26AS from incometax.gov.in (shows all TDS deducted in your name for the year). Salary slips showing TDS deducted (columns 'Basic + DA' and 'Tax deducted').
Form 16 from employer. Bank statement showing net salary credited (after TDS).
If TDS by multiple employers: Forms 16 from each.
For ITR processing delays:
ITR acknowledgment and ITR transcript (from portal under 'View Filed Returns'). Proof of payment if you paid tax with ITR (challan receipt).
Correspondence with IT helpline (if any).

sections.heading: Best Practices for Quick Resolution
content:
Be specific with dates:

Instead of 'refund pending,' write 'Refund of ₹50,000 for AY 2024-25 filed on 20 July 2024 via e-Filing, not received as of 27 March 2026.' Specific dates help CPC trace your record faster.

Mention relevant assessment year:

If you're complaining about demand for AY 2023-24, mention it clearly. The department handles thousands of grievances daily — AY clarity ensures your file is retrieved instantly.

One issue per grievance:

File separate grievances for separate issues. Don't combine 'refund pending + wrong demand' in one grievance — they require different investigations.
Multiple issues in one grievance can cause delays.

Attach all relevant documents:

CPC cannot contact you asking for more documents. Attach everything upfront.
If a document is missing, they'll close the grievance as 'cannot be resolved without further information.'

Check grievance status weekly:

Don't assume your grievance is forgotten. Log into the portal weekly and check 'Track Grievance.' Sometimes CPC needs clarification — they may reply asking for more info.
Respond within 7 days.

Keep PAN-bank linkage active:

If filing for refund, ensure your Aadhaar is linked with your bank account and PAN is linked with Aadhaar. Refund cannot be credited if bank details are incorrect.

sections.heading: External Links & Resources
content: Official Portals:
Income Tax e-Filing Portal (incometax.gov.in) — Login to file grievance.
e-Filing Portal (incometaxindiaefiling.gov.in) — Check ITR status and download Form 26AS.
Forms & Clarifications:
Income Tax Help Section — FAQs on refunds, demands, TDS, and e-Nivaran process.
Contact Support:
e-Nivaran Help: Support Portal (email grievance@incometax.gov.in).
Toll-free: 1800-180-1961 (8 AM - 8 PM, all days).

faqs.question: My refund is pending for 9 months. Should I file e-Nivaran now?
faqs.answer: Yes, immediately. Refunds should be credited within 30-45 days of ITR processing. If 9 months have passed, file e-Nivaran with: ITR acknowledgment, bank statement, and check your bank account IBAN in the ITR. Bank account mismatch is the #1 reason for delayed refunds. Many people file ITR with a closed bank account or wrong account number — the refund gets stuck. Correct your bank account on the portal, then file e-Nivaran.

faqs.question: I filed e-Nivaran 20 days ago, still no response. Should I file again?
faqs.answer: No, don't file again. The target is 30 days, and CPC is still within the timeline. Every new grievance restarts the 30-day counter and creates confusion. Check 'Track Grievance' status — if it shows 'Replied,' check the reply even if you didn't get an email notification. If status is still 'Under Investigation,' wait until day 30. If no response by day 31, escalate to the Grievance Officer or file RTI.

faqs.question: CPC replied that 'refund pending due to pending verification.' What does this mean?
faqs.answer: This means your ITR is still in 'Processing' stage, and CPC hasn't verified your details completely. Common reasons: (1) Bank account name doesn't match PAN name exactly (e.g., PAN: 'Rajesh Kumar' but bank: 'Raj Kumar'). (2) Aadhaar not linked with PAN. (3) Income details need clarification (e.g., source of income unclear). Reply to CPC's response with corrected information, or go to the nearest IT office to clarify in person.

faqs.question: What if CPC rejects my grievance saying 'no issue found'?
faqs.answer: If CPC finds no issue, it means: (1) Refund was already credited (check bank account again), (2) Demand was correct and legal, or (3) CPC couldn't find evidence of the issue you reported. If you still believe there's an issue, reply with additional evidence, or file an appeal to the Higher Authority. You can also file an RTI (Right to Information) query asking for details of your ITR processing and refund.

faqs.question: I'm non-resident (NRI). Can I file e-Nivaran?
faqs.answer: Yes, NRIs can file e-Nivaran using the same portal with their NRI PAN. If you have income in India (salary, rental, interest), you can file ITR and grievance. Submit documents in English. If you need help, contact NSDL's NRI helpline or work with a Chartered Accountant familiar with NRI taxation.

faqs.question: What if the grievance still isn't resolved after 30 days?
faqs.answer: If no response by day 30, escalate: (1) Reply to the grievance saying 'Response not received, requesting escalation to Grievance Officer.' (2) File an RTI with the CPC asking for your grievance file and reasons for delay. (3) Contact your local IT office or write to the Principal Commissioner of Income Tax. (4) File a complaint with the Income Tax Ombudsman if you believe there was wilful negligence. The Ombudsman can award compensation up to ₹1 lakh for unresolved grievances.

faqs.question: Will I need to visit an Income Tax office in person?
faqs.answer: Not for the basic grievance process. The entire e-Nivaran process is online, and you can track it from home. However, if CPC asks for 'original documents for verification' in their reply, you may need to visit the nearest IT office or submit documents by post. Most grievances are resolved entirely online without office visits.

faqs.question: Is there a fee for filing e-Nivaran?
faqs.answer: No, absolutely free. e-Nivaran is a free service provided by the Income Tax Department. Beware of fake websites claiming to file e-Nivaran for a fee — always use the official incometax.gov.in portal only.

disclaimer: This guide is for informational purposes based on Income Tax Department procedures as of March 2026. Tax laws, timelines, and procedures may change. Always verify current information from the official Income Tax Department website or consult with a Chartered Accountant for complex cases.
